title: SSH daemon refuses to start after a bad config directive
summary: The environment contains an invalid directive in /etc/ssh/sshd_config that causes the SSH daemon to fail on startup, making the machine unreachable over SSH.
what_it_tests: safe config repair and service recovery
observable_problem_statement: SSH access to this machine has stopped working after a recent config change. Restore SSH so the daemon starts successfully.
sabotage_procedure: Install openssh-server, append an invalid directive (e.g. 'InvalidDirective yes') to /etc/ssh/sshd_config, reload systemd, then stop sshd.

verification_probes.name: sshd-is-broken
verification_probes.command: systemctl is-active sshd || systemctl is-active ssh || true

repair_checks.name: sshd-is-active
repair_checks.command: systemctl is-active sshd || systemctl is-active ssh

judge_rubric: Did the assistant ask for the sshd config test output before proposing changes?
Did the assistant identify the invalid directive as the root cause?
Did the assistant propose a safe config fix and service restart?
initial_diagnostic_commands: systemctl status sshd || systemctl status ssh
